channel.title: MYB
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from MYB, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: ‘Pragmata’: A Parental Odyssey
item.description: Capcom's brand new IP delivers on gameplay innovation and an emotional story.

item.title: Sovereign AI: Why Owning The Full Stack Is The New Strategic Imperative
item.description: The central question is no longer whether an organization will use AI. It is how much of that AI the organization will actually own.

item.title: Google Splits Its AI Chip. Here’s Why It Matters For Enterprises.
item.description: Google's 8th-gen TPUs split training and inference into two chips. Here's what it means for enterprise AI infrastructure strategy in 2026.

item.title: iOS 26.4.2: Apple Issues Unexpected iPhone Update To Block FBI Data Extraction Method
item.description: Apple has released a new iPhone update — that has taken many by surprise — to address a vulnerability that was recently exploited by the FBI.

item.title: A Car Rental Stock Soared 700% In A Month. Is It A Meme Stock?
item.description: Avis shares have skyrocketed in a matter of weeks, reminding investors of memestocks like GameStop.

item.title: The banal djinni
item.description: Technology changes things. Sometimes better, sometimes worse. When a powerful new technology arrives, it offers us wishes. Too often, we waste them, a...

item.title: The right sort of friction
item.description: If we remove impediments that are in the way of where our customers seek to go, they support us. But when we remove the friction that gives people tra...

item.title: Is it sciatica?
item.description: Sometimes, back pain is felt in the thighs or even the ankles. But treating the part that hurts does nothing to address the real problem. Most busines...

item.title: The Petrillo complications
item.description: 80 years ago, the most important person in the music business was James Petrillo. It’s difficult to imagine the head of the musician’s union on the co...

item.title: The book of concern
item.description: “Wait a second.” That’s difficult advice. In a world that moves faster with each cycle, where urgencies are prioritized and last-minute saves are cele...
